@demarcusmiller7192  You’re right that misogyny and obsession can coexist – but that’s not what’s happening with Joe. His actions aren’t driven by the belief that women are inferior or deserve harm. He becomes obsessed with women he idealizes, tries to “save” them, and kills both men and women when they get in the way of his fantasy. That’s not misogyny – that’s pathological attachment and control rooted in trauma. Throwing the misogyny label on everything just waters down the term and ignores the actual psychology behind characters like Joe
